ruling: The third bullet point should read: "When a player is exhausting, sacrificing, or otherwise using cards to pay costs, only cards that are in play and under that player's control may be used..."

Add the following two bullet points:
- The collection exists outside of the game. Effects that search the collection cannot search in-game areas, such as decks or set-aside cards.
- If a player owns multiple copies of a single product, their collection is still only defined (for the purposes of game effects) as a single copy of each unique product they own.

Add the following, as step 0 (i.e. before step 1): "0. For the purpose of resolving weakness cards, the game has ended for the eliminated investigator. Trigger any 'when the game ends' abilities on each weakness the eliminated investigator owns that is in play. Then, remove those weaknesses from the game."

Add the following bullet point:
- A permanent card cannot have its text box blanked or treated as if it were blank.

The fifth bullet point should read: "If a weakness is added to a player's deck, hand, or threat area during the play of a scenario, that weakness remains a part of that investigator's deck for the rest of the campaign. (Unless it is removed from the campaign by a card ability or scenario resolution.)"
The fourth bullet point should read: "A card with the permanent keyword cannot leave play (except by elimination)."
Add the following bullet point: "Once added to your deck, permanent cards cannot be removed from your deck or swapped out of your deck unless explicitly stated otherwise."

The last paragraph should read: "If playing or gaining control of an asset would put an investigator above their slot limit for that type of asset, the investigator must choose and discard other assets under their control simultaneously with the new asset entering the slot."

The third bullet point (or sixth sentence, depending on your printing) should read: "A hidden card counts toward your hand size, but it cannot leave your hand by any means except those described on the card."
